title: The Victim
content:
I.

A plague upon the people fell,
A famine after laid them low;
Then thorpe and byre arose in fire,
For on them brake the sudden foe;
So thick they died the people cried,
ï¿½The Gods are moved against the land.ï¿½
The Priest in horror about his altar
To Thor and Odin lifted a hand:
ï¿½Help us from famine
And plague and strife!
What would you have of us?
Human life?
Were it our nearest,
Were it our dearest,ï¿½
Answer, O answer!ï¿½
We give you his life.ï¿½


II.

But still the foeman spoilï¿½d and burnï¿½d,
And cattle died, and deer in wood,
And bird in air, and fishes turnï¿½d
And whitenï¿½d all the rolling flood;
And dead men lay all over the way,
Or down in a furrow scathed with flame;
And ever and aye the Priesthood moanï¿½d,
Till at last it seemï¿½d that an answer came:
ï¿½The King is happy
In child and wife;
Take you his dearest,
Give us a life.ï¿½


III.

The Priest went out by heath and hill;
The King was hunting in the wild;
They found the mother sitting still;
She cast her arms about the child.
The child was only eight summers old,
His beauty still with his years increased,
His face was ruddy, his hair was gold;
He seemï¿½d a victim due to the priest.
The Priest beheld him,
And cried with joy,
ï¿½The Gods have answerï¿½d;
We give them the boy.ï¿½


IV.

The King returnï¿½d from out the wild,
He bore but little game in hand;
The mother said, ï¿½They have taken the child
To spill his blood and heal the land.
The land is sick, the people diseased,
And blight and famine on all the lea;
The holy Gods, they must be appeased,
So I pray you tell the truth to me.
They have taken our son,
They will have his life.
Is he your dearest?
Or I, the wife?ï¿½


V.

The King bent low, with hand on brow,
He stayï¿½d his arms upon his knee:
ï¿½O wife, what use to answer now?
For now the Priest has judged for me.ï¿½
The King was shaken with holy fear;
ï¿½The Gods,ï¿½ he said, ï¿½would have chosen well;
Yet both are near, and both are dear,
And which the dearest I cannot tell!ï¿½
But the Priest was happy,
His victim won:
ï¿½We have his dearest,
His only son!ï¿½


VI.

The rites prepared, the victim bared,
The knife uprising toward the blow,
To the altar-stone she sprang alone:
ï¿½Me, not my darling, no!ï¿½
He caught her away with a sudden cry;
Suddenly from him brake his wife,
And shrieking, ï¿½I am his dearest, Iï¿½
I am his dearest!ï¿½ rushï¿½d on the knife.
And the Priest was happy:
ï¿½O Father Odin,
We give you a life.
Which was his nearest?
Who was his dearest?
The Gods have answerï¿½d;
We give them the wife!ï¿½
meaning:
This evocative piece by Alfred Lord Tennyson, titled "The Victim", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
